audit_items.proof_trace: `ldap_url_parse` internally uses `LDAP_PVT_URL_PARSE_HISTORIC` which includes `LDAP_PVT_URL_PARSE_NOEMPTY_HOST`; when parsing a URL with an empty host (e.g., `ldapi:///`), `lud_host` is explicitly freed and set to NULL (url.c:992-998) while the parse still returns `LDAP_URL_SUCCESS`. Lines 84 and 88 of `urltest.c` then pass `lud->lud_host` (NULL) directly to `fprintf` as a `%s` argument without any NULL guard, which is undefined behavior in C and can crash on non-Linux platforms. Additionally, `ldap_url_desc2str(lud)` at line 125 can return NULL on allocation failure and is also passed unchecked to `fprintf`.
audit_items.attack_scenario: Run `urltest ldapi:///`; `ldap_url_parse` returns success with `lud->lud_host == NULL`, then `fprintf(stdout, "PATH: %s\n", NULL)` is called at line 84, triggering undefined behavior (crash on macOS/BSD/Windows).
